content.problemStatement:
Chef has two arrays AAA and BBB of the same size NNN.

In one operation, Chef can:


Choose an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1‚â§i‚â§N) and swap the elements AiA_iAi‚Äã and BiB_iBi‚Äã.
Chef came up with a task to find the **minimum** possible value of **(Amax‚àíAminA_{max} - A_{min}Amax‚Äã‚àíAmin‚Äã)** after performing the swap operation any (possibly zero) number of times.

Since Chef is busy, can you help him solve this task?

Note that AmaxA_{max}Amax‚Äã and AminA_{min}Amin‚Äã denote the maximum and minimum elements of the array AAA respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚Äî the number of elements in each array.
The second line consists of NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the elements of the array AAA.
The third line consists of NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots ,B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã denoting the elements of the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** possible value of **(Amax‚àíAminA_{max} - A_{min}Amax‚Äã‚àíAmin‚Äã)** in the array AAA after doing swap operation any number of times.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can make the following operations:

Operation 111: Choose i=1i=1i=1 and swap A1A_1A1‚Äã with B1B_1B1‚Äã.

By doing the above operations, array AAA becomes [2,2][2, 2][2,2]. Here (Amax‚àíAmin)=0(A_{max} - A_{min}) = 0(Amax‚Äã‚àíAmin‚Äã)=0. It can be shown that this is the minimum value possible.
Test case 222: Chef can make the following operations:

Operation 111: Choose i=1i=1i=1 and swap A1A_1A1‚Äã with B1B_1B1‚Äã.
Operation 222: Choose i=2i=2i=2 and swap A2A_2A2‚Äã with B2B_2B2‚Äã.

By doing the above operations, array AAA becomes [2,3,3][2, 3, 3][2,3,3]. Here (Amax‚àíAmin)=1(A_{max} - A_{min}) = 1(Amax‚Äã‚àíAmin‚Äã)=1. It can be shown that this is the minimum value possible.
Test case 333: Chef can make the following operations:

Operation 111: Choose i=2i=2i=2 and swap A2A_2A2‚Äã with B2B_2B2‚Äã.
Operation 222: Choose i=3i=3i=3 and swap A3A_3A3‚Äã with B3B_3B3‚Äã.

By doing the above operations, array AAA becomes [4,3,4,1][4, 3, 4, 1][4,3,4,1]. Here (Amax‚àíAmin)=3(A_{max} - A_{min}) = 3(Amax‚Äã‚àíAmin‚Äã)=3. It can be shown that this is the minimum value possible.
